name: sen life insurance
facts: The Policyowner has the right to cancel this policy at any time.
Cancellation of the policy requires a written request.
Insurance coverage terminates upon cancellation of the policy.
No further benefits will be payable after the policy is canceled.
Unearned premiums will be refunded if the policy has any.
The Policyowner has the right to elect the method by which the Death Benefit is paid.
The Policyowner may change the method by which the Death Benefit is paid.
Any election or change in the method of Death Benefit payment is subject to approval by the Company.
The settlement options available are governed by the terms offered by the Company.
The Policyowner may assign this policy by submitting a written assignment acceptable to the Company.
The Policyowner may assign any interest in the policy by submitting a written assignment acceptable to the Company.
The Company shall not be responsible for the validity of any assignment.
The Company shall not be responsible for the tax consequences of any assignment.
The rights of the Owner are subject to the terms of the assignment.
The rights of the Beneficiary are subject to the terms of the assignment.
The Policyowner has the right to change the Beneficiary at any time during the Insured's lifetime.
A written request must be submitted to the Company to change the Beneficiary.
The change in Beneficiary takes effect as of the date the request is signed.
The change is effective even if the Insured is not alive when the Company receives the request.
The Policyowner has the right to request a copy of the policy.
The Policyowner has the right to request premium payment history.
The Policyowner has the right to request beneficiary designations.
The Policyowner has the right to request any applicable riders or endorsements.
The Company will provide the requested information within a reasonable time.
The Company must provide the requested information in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.
This policy, together with the application and any attached riders or endorsements, constitutes the entire contract between the Policy Owner and the Company.
The application is part of the entire contract between the Policy Owner and the Company.
Any attached riders or endorsements are part of the entire contract between the Policy Owner and the Company.
No agent or representative may alter any provision of this policy.
No agent or representative may waive any provision of this policy.
An agent or representative may only alter a provision of this policy if expressly authorized in writing by an officer of the Company.
An agent or representative may only waive a provision of this policy if expressly authorized in writing by an officer of the Company.
Written authorization from an officer of the Company is required to alter or waive any provision of this policy.
This policy does not include an Automatic Premium Loan provision unless endorsed or added by rider.
An Automatic Premium Loan provision can be added to the policy through a rider.
If an Automatic Premium Loan is in force, unpaid premiums are paid automatically by a policy loan.
The Automatic Premium Loan is triggered at the end of the grace period.
A policy loan is used to pay unpaid premiums only if there is sufficient loan value available.
The availability of loan value determines whether the Automatic Premium Loan can be applied.
The Accelerated Death Benefit Rider allows for early payment of a portion of the Death Benefit if the Insured is diagnosed with a terminal illness with a life expectancy of 12 months or less.
The amount payable under the rider shall not exceed 50% of the policy's Face Amount.
The payment of an accelerated benefit is subject to a maximum limit determined by the Company.
To receive an accelerated benefit, the Policyowner must submit a written request and provide medical documentation acceptable to the Company.
The Company reserves the right to obtain a second medical opinion at its own expense.
Upon payment of an accelerated benefit, the Death Benefit is reduced by the amount paid, including any applicable administrative charges and interest.
Any outstanding loans or liens on the policy are deducted when an accelerated benefit is paid.
No accelerated benefit will be paid if the terminal illness results from intentional self-inflicted injury, participation in a felony, or war-related activities.
The Policyowner may revoke the request for accelerated benefits at any time before the benefit is paid by providing written notice to the Company.
This policy is executed by Sentinel Life Assurance Company at its Home Office in Hartford, Connecticut.
The policy becomes effective on the date shown in the Schedule of Benefits, provided the first premium has been paid and the Insured is living on that date.
The policy form number is SLAC-TL2025-20YR.
The policy may include additional state-specific notices or provisions as required by law.
Policyholders should refer to attached endorsements or state notice pages for additional state-specific information.
Any person who knowingly provides materially false information or conceals material facts in an insurance application or claim commits a fraudulent insurance act.
Jordan Everett Hughes is the insured under the policy.
The policy number is SLAC-2025-001.
The policy type is Level Term Life Insurance for 20 years.
Jordan Everett Hughes is 37 years old at the time of issue.
Jordan Everett Hughes is male.
Jordan Everett Hughes is classified as a standard non-smoker.
The face amount of the policy is $500,000.
The initial premium is $42.00.
The premium payment mode is monthly via bank draft.
The premium is guaranteed not to increase for 20 years.
The coverage term runs from August 7, 2025, to August 7, 2045.
Elaine M. Hughes is the primary beneficiary.
Morgan L. Hughes is the contingent beneficiary.
An Accelerated Death Benefit Rider is attached to the policy.
The reinstatement period is 5 years from the date of lapse.
Premiums are level and guaranteed during the guaranteed term period.
Coverage will terminate at the end of the term unless renewed or converted as specified in the contract.
All benefits are subject to the terms and exclusions outlined in the policy.
The Policy Owner has the right to change the Beneficiary designation at any time.
A written request must be submitted to the Company to change the Beneficiary.
The change in Beneficiary takes effect as of the date the request is signed.
The change is effective regardless of whether the Insured is alive when the Company receives the request.
This Individual Term Life Insurance contract includes the application, policy schedule, riders, and endorsements.
Sentinel Life Assurance Company is the Company referred to in the policy.
The Insured is the person whose life is covered under this policy.
The Owner is the person who has all rights of ownership under the policy.
The Beneficiary is the person(s) designated to receive the Death Benefit upon the Insured's death.
The Death Benefit is the amount payable to the Beneficiary upon the Insured's death, subject to the terms of this policy.
The Premium is the payment required to keep the policy in force.
The Grace Period is a 31-day period following a missed premium during which the policy remains in force.
Reinstatement is the process by which a lapsed policy may be restored to active status.
The Incontestability Period is a two-year period during which the Company may contest the validity of the policy based on material misrepresentations.
A lapsed policy may be reinstated within five years of lapse.
Reinstatement requires providing evidence of insurability satisfactory to the Company.
All past due premiums must be paid for reinstatement.
Interest on past due premiums is determined by the Company at the time of reinstatement.
The Company has the authority to determine what constitutes satisfactory evidence of insurability.
The reinstatement period is limited to five years from the date of lapse.
Payment of past due premiums is a mandatory condition for reinstatement.
The Company has discretion in setting the interest rate applied to past due premiums.
This policy is issued in consideration of the statements made in the application.
This policy is issued in consideration of the payment of the first premium.
Continued coverage is subject to the timely payment of all subsequent premiums due.
The first premium must be paid for the policy to be issued.
Subsequent premiums must be paid on time to maintain coverage.
The application statements are a factor in issuing the policy.
The payment of the first premium is a condition for policy issuance.
Failure to pay subsequent premiums on time may result in loss of coverage.
The Company will pay the Death Benefit to the designated Beneficiary upon receipt of due proof of the Insured's death while the policy is in force.
The Death Benefit is typically paid in a lump sum unless an alternative settlement option is elected and approved by the Company.
Acceptable proof of death includes a certified copy of the death certificate and any other documentation the Company may reasonably require.
The Company will pay the Death Benefit promptly upon receipt of satisfactory proof of death.
Interest, as required by applicable law, will accrue from the date of death until the date of payment.
The Beneficiary is the person or entity named in the application or most recent written designation on file with the Company.
If no Beneficiary survives the Insured, the Death Benefit will be paid to the Policy Owner if they are living.
If the Policy Owner does not survive the Insured, the Death Benefit will be paid to the Owner's estate.
The Policy Owner possesses all contractual rights.
The Policy Owner has the right to change beneficiaries.
The Policy Owner has the right to assign the policy.
The Policy Owner has the right to request cancellation.
If no Owner is named, the Insured shall be deemed the Owner.
The Owner has the right to examine the policy for 20 days from receipt.
The examination period begins on the day the policy is received.
If the Owner is not satisfied, the policy may be returned to the Company.
A full refund of any premium paid will be issued upon return of the policy.
Upon return, the policy shall be void from inception.
The policy will be treated as if it was never issued after return.
This policy shall be incontestable after it has been in force during the lifetime of the Insured for two years from the Effective Date, except for nonpayment of premium.
Any misrepresentations or omissions that materially affect the acceptance of risk may render the policy void if discovered within the first two years.
If the Insured dies by suicide within two years from the Effective Date, the Company's liability is limited to a refund of premiums paid, without interest, less any indebtedness.
If the age or gender of the Insured has been misstated, the amount of insurance shall be adjusted to the amount that the premium paid would have purchased at the correct age or gender.
A grace period of 31 days is granted for the payment of each premium after the first.
During the grace period, the policy remains in force.
If the Insured dies during the grace period, any unpaid premium due will be deducted from the Death Benefit.

If a premium is not paid before the end of the grace period, the policy shall lapse as of the due date of the unpaid premium, and coverage shall terminate with no further benefits payable, except as provided under the Reinstatement provision.
The first premium is due on the Effective Date of the policy.
Subsequent premiums are due on each premium due date according to the payment mode selected.
The Policy Owner may request a change in the frequency or method of premium payment by written notice to the Company.
A change in premium payment frequency or method becomes effective upon approval by the Company.
Failure to pay the premium by the end of the grace period will cause the policy to lapse.
Policy lapse results in termination of coverage without value.
Coverage termination without value is subject to the Reinstatement provision.
The policy number is SLAC-2025-001.
The insurer is Sentinel Life Assurance Company.
The principal office of the insurer is located at 101 Guardian Way, Hartford, CT 06103.
The insured person is Jordan Everett Hughes.
The policy owner is Jordan Everett Hughes.
The effective date of the policy is August 7, 2025.
The policy type is Level Term Life Insurance for 20 years.
The contract is governed by the laws of the State of Colorado.
This policy is a legal contract between the policy owner and Sentinel Life Assurance Company.
The company promises to pay the death benefit to the named beneficiary, subject to the terms and conditions of the policy.
The policy, along with the application and any attached riders or endorsements, constitutes the entire contract.
Any changes to the policy must be made in writing and signed by an officer of the company.
The policy includes a two-year contestability period.
The policy includes a suicide exclusion clause.
The policy was signed by Eleanor B. Raynor, Chief Executive Officer, on the effective date.
The premium amount is shown in the Schedule of Benefits.
The payment mode for premiums is shown in the Schedule of Benefits.
Premiums are payable in advance.
The Policy Owner can elect the payment frequency for premiums.
The Company must approve the elected payment frequency for premiums.
Premiums may be paid on a monthly basis.
Premiums may be paid on a quarterly basis.
Premiums may be paid on a semi-annual basis.
Premiums may be paid on an annual basis.
The policy may be modified by the insurer.
The policy provisions can be waived by the insurer.
The insurer has the authority to waive any of the policy provisions.
The policy provisions are subject to change by the insurer.
Any provision of the policy can be waived at the discretion of the insurer.
The Effective Date of this policy is shown on the Schedule of Benefits.
Coverage begins at 12:01 A.M. Standard Time.
The start time of coverage is at the Owner's address.
The date when coverage begins is the Effective Date.
No change or waiver of any provision in this policy is valid unless made in writing.
A change or waiver of any provision in this policy must be signed by an authorized officer of the Company.
An authorized officer of the Company must sign any written change or waiver.
No agent is authorized to modify this policy.
No broker is authorized to modify this policy.
